home  

Below, you will find a list of my publications in chronological order. Of course, this information can also be found on my  google scholar .
At present, the flavor of my published research reflects the work I did in a past life as an economics pre-doctoral student. More research typical of a statistics PhD student is in the works.

title: Oliver Hannaoui
text:
research   personal projects   musings   more musings   fun


Oliver Hannaoui

Greetings! I am a first-year Statistics PhD student at Carnegie Mellon University. I am also a member of the Delphi group, where I work on projects related to infectious disease forecasting.
Previously, I spent a year in Paris where I completed an M1 in Applied Mathematics and Statistics at the Polytechnic Institute of Paris. During my time in Paris, I also completed an AI engineering internship at Ennov.
Prior to that, I spent two formative years in New York City at the Federal Reserve Bank of New York as a Senior Research Analyst in the Financial Intermediation group. I graduated with bachelor’s degrees in Economics and Statistics from the University of California, Davis. 
I can be contacted via e-mail or LinkedIn .


Raouché, located in Beirut Lebanon.
